description:
Advances: specled.coverage_capture

lib/specled_ex/coverage/store.ex: v2 envelope %{version, mode, generated_at, source, files, mfas, payload, degraded}; read/1 -> {:ok, env} | {:error, :legacy_artifact | :invalid_artifact}; refuse writing empty-files envelopes; read_status/1 for the last_run.status sidecar ({:ok, stats} | {:refused, reason}) written on every flush/ingest. Decision 5: legacy artifacts are REJECTED with a clear message naming the re-run command (mix spec.cover.test) — no auto-migrate/delete. Include envelope round-trip tests + legacy-rejection tests.

## Compatibility constraint (orchestrator)

Every ticket merges into the epic branch independently and the merge gate runs mix compile --warnings-as-errors, mix test, mix format --check-formatted, mix deps.unlock --check-unused, and mix spec.check after each close. Existing callers of Store (Coverage.Formatter flush, CoverageTriangulation, Review.CoverageClosure, mix spec.triangle) are NOT in this ticket's scope — introduce the v2 envelope contract additively so unchanged callers keep compiling and their tests keep passing. Caller migration happens in specled_-155.4/.5 (formatter) and specled_-155.6 (readers). If the literal ticket text conflicts with keeping the tree green, additive wins; note the tension in your completion record rather than expanding scope.

## Spec co-change (orchestrator)

specled dogfoods itself: if your diff drifts realization hashes or contradicts a must-requirement in .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md, co-edit ONLY the requirement text/stubs your change directly invalidates (the wholesale spec rewrite is specled_-155.8). mix spec.check must be green in your worktree before completion.

## Allowed touches

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/store.ex
- test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs
- .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md (only sections your diff invalidates + stub flips)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated by mix spec.check)

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex
- lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex
- lib/specled_ex/review/**
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- docs/**

## Out of Scope (intent)

- No new external deps
- No behavior change for existing v1 write/read callers in this ticket
- No formatter or mix-task edits

## Verification

- mix test test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs green
- Envelope round-trip test: write v2 -> read v2 returns identical envelope
- read/1 on a pre-v2 artifact returns {:error, :legacy_artifact} with a message naming mix spec.cover.test
- read/1 on garbage returns {:error, :invalid_artifact}
- write refuses an empty-files envelope
- read_status/1 returns {:ok, stats} | {:refused, reason} from the last_run.status sidecar
- mix spec.check green (with any co-changed spec sections)

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.1/codex-impl-1/log

Commit: 14a22d46200ee5ea6046959764f03ab6400993c4
Files touched:
  lib/specled_ex/coverage/store.ex
  test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs
  .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md
  .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-seeded by mix spec.check, additive-only:
    4 new entries for the new v2 functions; the 3 pre-existing Store entries
    are untouched at their original committed values)
Tests added: SpecLedEx.Coverage.StoreTest
  build_envelope/1: defaults version/generated_at/degraded; raises KeyError
    on missing required field
  v2 envelope round trip: write_v2 -> read_v2 identical envelope; write_v2
    refuses empty-files (returns {:error, :empty_files}, no file written);
    write_v2 creates parent dir; read_v2 on legacy (v1) artifact returns
    {:error, :legacy_artifact, message} naming `mix spec.cover.test`; read_v2
    on garbage bytes / non-envelope map / missing file returns
    {:error, :invalid_artifact}
  read_status/1: {:ok, stats} after success, {:refused, :empty_files} after
    a refused write, {:refused, :not_found} when no sidecar exists yet
Verification stubs flipped: none pre-existing; added a new tagged_tests
  verification block in coverage_capture.spec.md covering
  specled.coverage_capture.store_v2_envelope and
  specled.coverage_capture.store_v2_legacy_rejection (both execute: true)
Verification tier: change-scoped — ran
  test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs (18 tests),
  test/specled_ex/coverage/formatter_test.exs,
  test/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation_test.exs,
  test/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure_test.exs,
  test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs,
  test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs (52 tests total, 0 failures) to
  confirm existing v1 callers are unaffected, plus `mix compile
  --warnings-as-errors`, `mix format --check-formatted`, and both
  `mix spec.check --no-run-commands` and full `mix spec.check` (command
  execution included) — all green. Full-system proof deferred to the
  orchestrator's merge gate.
Final-tier runs: 3 (first run surfaced a test-fixture bug on my end —
  missing :payload field in one of my own build_envelope/1 test cases, fixed
  immediately; second run surfaced real api_boundary realization drift on
  build_records/1, read/1, write/2 requiring a diff restructure, described
  below; third run and all spec.check runs after that were green)

Design notes / tensions:
- Ticket text says "read/1 -> {:ok, env} | {:error, ...}" but the
  Compatibility constraint requires v1 read/1 (raw list return, no tuple,
  used unwrapped by CoverageTriangulation and Review.CoverageClosure, and
  wrapped as `{:ok, Store.read(path)}` by mix spec.triangle) to keep its
  exact current behavior. Reusing the name read/1 for v2 would break those
  callers immediately. Resolved per the ticket's own guidance ("additive
  wins; note the tension") by adding new functions read_v2/1, write_v2/2
  instead of overloading read/1 or write/2. v1 write/2 and read/1 are
  byte-for-byte unchanged.
- read_v2/1's legacy-artifact error is a 3-tuple {:error, :legacy_artifact,
  message} rather than a bare 2-tuple, so the message naming
  `mix spec.cover.test` (required by Decision 5 and the Verification list)
  is part of the return value rather than only a side-channel log.
- files/mfas/payload entry shapes are left opaque (typed as [term()] /
  term()) rather than pinned to a concrete per-entry schema — that's
  Aggregate.ingest/2's contract (specled_-155.2), not this ticket's.
- v2 targets the SAME default on-disk path as v1
  (.spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata via Store.default_path/0), by design:
  that's what makes "legacy artifact left over at that path" a real,
  detectable case rather than a hypothetical one.
- Discovered (not fixed — out of Allowed touches) a real bug in
  lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex: strip_meta/1 fails to recurse
  into the leading `form` element of a 3-tuple AST node, so remote-call
  guards (`is_binary`, `is_list`, etc., which expand to `:erlang.*` dotted
  calls) leak their source line/column into the api_boundary hash despite
  the module's own documented "stable under line-number shifts" contract.
  Any diff that shifts lines above a guarded function anywhere in the repo
  can trigger spurious branch_guard_realization_drift findings. Worked
  around here by restructuring store.ex so all new v2 code is appended
  after the existing v1 functions (zero lines shifted above
  build_records/1, write/2, read/1) instead of editing the moduledoc/top of
  the file. Filed as specled_-wp7 (bug, advances specled.api_boundary) with
  full repro details for whoever picks it up — worth being aware of for
  every remaining ticket in this epic that touches bound files.
Deferred must requirements: none — all Verification checklist items
  delivered in this ticket.

Summary: Added a versioned v2 envelope container to
SpecLedEx.Coverage.Store (build_envelope/1, write_v2/2, read_v2/1,
read_status/1) alongside the unchanged v1 write/2 and read/1, satisfying
every item in the ticket's Verification list: envelope round-trip,
legacy-artifact rejection naming the re-run command, invalid-artifact
rejection, empty-files write refusal, and a last_run.status sidecar. v1
callers (Formatter, triangulation, review, spec.triangle) are untouched and
their tests still pass. mix spec.check is green in the worktree, with the
coverage_capture spec co-changed additively (two new requirements) rather
than rewritten, per the epic's Spec co-change policy.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: verify-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.1/codex-verify-1/log

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 4 (lib/specled_ex/coverage/store.ex, test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs, .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md, .spec/realization_hashes.json)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 (tagged_tests execute:true for store_v2_envelope + store_v2_legacy_rejection), passed: 1 — executed under `mix spec.check` (command execution on) and directly via the store test.
- Requirements coverage (this ticket's blast radius = v2 envelope additions):
  - specled.coverage_capture.store_v2_envelope: covered by test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs — build_envelope defaults (:96), write_v2->read_v2 identical round trip (:136), write_v2 refuses empty :files + no file written (:151), parent-dir creation (:165), read_status {:ok, stats} (:233), {:refused, :empty_files} (:252), {:refused, :not_found} (:266). Would fail if the envelope did not round-trip byte-identical, if an empty-files envelope were written instead of refused, or if the sidecar were not (re)written on every write_v2 call. Seams: none (real File IO to System.tmp_dir! paths; no Mock/Memory/fake seams).
  - specled.coverage_capture.store_v2_legacy_rejection: covered by store_test.exs — v1 bare-list artifact -> {:error, :legacy_artifact, message} with message =~ "mix spec.cover.test" (:185), garbage bytes -> {:error, :invalid_artifact} (:194), well-formed non-envelope map -> :invalid_artifact (:201), missing file -> :invalid_artifact (:210). Would fail if a v1 list were misclassified as valid/invalid instead of legacy, if the message dropped the re-run command, or if undecodable/malformed content did not resolve to :invalid_artifact. Seams: none.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. mix.exs untouched (no new external deps); no formatter/mix-task/coverage.ex/triangulation/review edits; v1 write/2 and read/1 are byte-for-byte unchanged (store.ex diff is insertion-only, 208 additions / 0 deletions; realization hashes for build_records/1, write/2, read/1 unchanged, 4 new entries added for the new functions).
- Targeted verification: PASS. mix test test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs = 18 tests, 0 failures. mix spec.check exit 0 (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, findings=0, single_subject specled.coverage_capture). mix compile --warnings-as-errors exit 0. mix format --check-formatted (touched files) exit 0.
- Corpus-scoped claims: n/a for store.ex/test (code diff). Spec-doc claims validated: 4 new api_boundary identifiers (build_envelope/1, write_v2/2, read_v2/1, read_status/1) all exist in store.ex and pass api_boundary realization under spec.check; the re-run command `mix spec.cover.test` named in the legacy message and spec text is a real registered Mix task (mix help spec.cover.test exit 0). No falsehoods found.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design).
- Audit-expanded scope: none.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Additive-design tension (already flagged by implementer, authorized by the ticket's Compatibility constraint "additive wins"): ticket header phrases the contract as read/1 -> {:ok, env} | {:error, ...}, but v1 read/1 must keep its raw-list return for unmigrated callers, so the v2 contract was added as read_v2/1 + write_v2/2 rather than overloading read/1. This matches the epic's explicit guidance; not a gap.
- read_v2/1's legacy return is a 3-tuple {:error, :legacy_artifact, message} rather than a 2-tuple, so the required re-run message is in-band. The ticket Verification asks for {:error, :legacy_artifact} "with a message naming mix spec.cover.test" — the 3-tuple satisfies this.
- Advisory (out of this ticket's Allowed touches, already filed by implementer as specled_-wp7): a strip_meta/1 recursion gap in lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex. Not touched here; the implementer appended all v2 code after existing functions to avoid line shifts. No follow-up needed from this pass.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: 0a6853df-a850-4f0b-9cf5-09b2923d30b8 (orchestrator)
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mzubieta-dev-mz-specled-ex/0a6853df-a850-4f0b-9cf5-09b2923d30b8.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full stub and requirement coverage, zero
scope violations, passing targeted verification, complete seam disclosure, and
no seam substituting production wiring. No auditor was spawned per policy.

title: T1: Store v2 envelope, validation, legacy rejection, last_run.status sidecar
